name: System.Value_U_Spec

documentation.description: Maximum value of exponent for 10 that fits in Uns'Base

record_types.name: Split_Value_Ghost
Uns_Option

subprograms.name: Exponent_Unsigned_Ghost

documentation.description: Ghost function that recursively computes Value * Base ** Exp

subprograms.name: Hexa_To_Unsigned_Ghost
subprograms.code: function Hexa_To_Unsigned_Ghost (X : Character) return Uns

documentation.description:
Ghost function that computes the value corresponding to an hexadecimal
digit.

subprograms.name: Last_Hexa_Ghost
subprograms.code: function Last_Hexa_Ghost (Str : String) return Positive

documentation.description:
Ghost function that returns the index of the last character in S that
is either an hexadecimal digit or an underscore, which necessarily
exists given the precondition on Str.

subprograms.name: Lemma_Exponent_Unsigned_Ghost_Base

documentation.description: Base case: Exponent_Unsigned_Ghost returns 0 if Value or Exp is 0

subprograms.name: Lemma_Exponent_Unsigned_Ghost_Overflow

documentation.description: Overflow case: the next multiplication overflows

subprograms.name: Lemma_Exponent_Unsigned_Ghost_Step
Lemma_Scan_Based_Number_Ghost_Base

documentation.description: Base case: Scan_Based_Number_Ghost returns Acc if From is bigger than To

subprograms.name: Lemma_Scan_Based_Number_Ghost_Overflow

documentation.description: Overflow case: scanning a digit which causes an overflow

subprograms.name: Lemma_Scan_Based_Number_Ghost_Step

documentation.description: Normal case: scanning a digit without overflows

subprograms.name: Lemma_Scan_Based_Number_Ghost_Underscore

documentation.description: Underscore case: underscores are ignored while scanning

subprograms.name: Max_Log10
subprograms.code: function Max_Log10 return Natural

subprograms.name: Prove_Scan_Based_Number_Ghost_Eq

documentation.description:
Scan_Based_Number_Ghost returns the same value on two slices which are
equal.

subprograms.name: Prove_Scan_Only_Decimal_Ghost

documentation.description:
Ghost lemma used in the proof of 'Image implementation, to prove that
the result of Value_Unsigned on a decimal string is the same as the
result of Scan_Based_Number_Ghost.

subprograms.name: Raw_Unsigned_Is_Based_Ghost

documentation.description: Return True if Str is a based number

subprograms.name: Raw_Unsigned_Starts_As_Based_Ghost
subprograms.code:
function Raw_Unsigned_Starts_As_Based_Ghost
  (Str               : String;
   Last_Num_Init, To : Integer)
   return Boolean

documentation.description: Return True if Str starts as a based number

subprograms.name: Scan_Based_Number_Ghost

documentation.description:
Ghost function that recursively computes the based number in Str,
assuming Acc has been scanned already and scanning continues at index
From.

subprograms.name: Scan_Overflows_Ghost

documentation.description:
Ghost function which returns True if Digit + Base * Acc overflows or
Digit is greater than Base, as this is used by the algorithm for the
test of overflow.

subprograms.name: Slide_If_Necessary
subprograms.code: function Slide_If_Necessary (Str : String) return String

documentation.description: If Str'Last = Positive'Last then slides Str so that it starts at 1

subprograms.name: Slide_To_1
subprograms.code: function Slide_To_1 (Str : String) return String

documentation.description: Slides Str so that it starts at 1

subprograms.name: Wrap_Option
subprograms.code: function Wrap_Option (Value : Uns) return Uns_Option
